title: Remote spawn — SPAWN_REQUEST/REPLY across nodes
purpose: SPAWN_REQUEST allows a process on node A to spawn a process on node B. The request carries the MFA and options; the reply carries the new PID. This enables distributed supervision trees where supervisors spawn children on remote nodes.
task: Implement SPAWN_REQUEST and SPAWN_REPLY control messages.

requirements.title: SPAWN_REQUEST handler
requirements.spec: Handle incoming SPAWN_REQUEST: spawn the process locally and reply with the new PID.
acceptance: Incoming SPAWN_REQUEST decoded: {ReqId, From, GroupLeader, {M,F,A}, OptList}
Spawns the process on the local scheduler
Sends SPAWN_REPLY with the new PID back to the requesting node
Test: remote spawn request creates a local process

requirements.title: Remote spawn BIF
requirements.spec: Register erlang:spawn/4 variant that specifies a target node.
acceptance: spawn(Node, Module, Function, Args) sends SPAWN_REQUEST to target node
Blocks (or uses async callback) until SPAWN_REPLY received
spawn_link and spawn_monitor variants include link/monitor options in the request
Test: spawn process on remote node, verify PID is node-qualified

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement spawn_opt across nodes with all options — only link and monitor
SHALL NOT implement node selection strategies — caller specifies the exact target node
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
